item.title: Version 2.9
item.description:
cyberWriter 2.9

Native PDF Viewer

  • Open .pdf files in a dedicated read-only viewer window — no more bouncing out to Preview
  • Triggered from Finder (double-click), the vault sidebar, or by dropping a PDF on the app chrome
  • PDFs dropped directly into the editor still embed as before — the editor's drop handler wins on its own area
  • Powered by PDFKit's PDFView: continuous-scroll, autoscale, page breaks, search, and thumbnail navigation come for free
  • Each PDF gets its own window, independent of any Markdown editor — close the editor without losing your reference docs

Configurable Line Spacing

  • New "Preview & Export Typography" setting in Preferences → Export controls line height for the live preview and PDF / Word / HTML exports
  • Discrete presets: Theme default, 1.0, 1.15, 1.25, 1.5, 1.75, 2.0, 2.5 — matches Word/Pages, no slider ambiguity
  • Per-document override via YAML frontmatter — drops in front of the global setting:
---
title: My Doc
line-height: 1.7
---
  • Editor pane intentionally untouched — keeps tracking the system default for fastest typing feel

AI Chat: Continuity Across Windows

  • New Document (⌘N), New Tab (⌘T), and Open... (⌘O) now carry your active AI conversation into the new window
  • Previously only vault wikilink clicks did this — now the menu paths match
  • Both windows stay synced via the shared conversation manager — type in either, see it in both

Reliability

  • Vault embeddings: eliminated the first-launch flashing loop when Apple Intelligence is off, still downloading, or warming up — the indexing banner now appears once and only when there's real work to do
  • Embedding builds persist progress every 25 files instead of 100, so an interrupted build resumes from where it left off
  • Sparkle auto-updates fire predictably the moment you accept the permission prompt — no more waiting until tomorrow for the first check
  • Sparkle check interval lowered from 24h to 1h so updates land within an hour of release
  • Update checks now flow from GitHub Pages going forward — same EdDSA signature key, no action required, future releases land automatically

Polish

  • Knowledge Graph: thinner edge widths so dense graphs read less crowded, especially at low zoom
  • AI Quick Actions: "POPULAR" badge restyled in normal case for visual consistency with surrounding chrome

item.title: Version 2.8
item.description:
cyberWriter 2.8

Chat with Your Vault

  • On-device semantic embeddings via Apple's NLContextualEmbedding — your notes, indexed locally, never sent anywhere
  • AI answers grounded in your own vault: ask a question and the top matching chunks are spliced into context automatically
  • Per-conversation toggle in the AI panel and a global switch in AI Settings
  • Optional vault structure summary so the AI knows every file that exists, even ones it didn't directly retrieve
  • Works with every provider — Apple Intelligence, Claude, OpenAI, Ollama, LM Studio
  • Vault Map report — see exactly what was embedded, when, and why files were skipped

Concept Search

  • Vault search bar now surfaces a "Related Ideas" section beneath exact matches
  • Search "spaceship" and find notes about orbits, rockets, and trajectories — even when the word never appears
  • Auto-updates as you edit files, with a Build / Rebuild / Clear panel in AI Settings

Rendered AI Responses

  • AI chat now renders markdown — headings, lists, code blocks, bold/italic
  • Vault filenames in responses become clickable wikilinks that open the file
  • Toggle back to plain text in the AI panel header if you prefer the raw view

Feature Tour

  • Interactive spotlight tour walks new users through every toolbar feature
  • Available from the welcome screen and the Help menu, anytime
  • First-launch users land in a populated UI with vault and AI panel open

Polish

  • New documents (Cmd+N, +Tab, templates) reliably inherit the source window's sidebar layout
  • Apple Intelligence: clear status detection on macOS 26+ with a one-click deep link to System Settings if it's installed but disabled
  • Knowledge Graph node labels render above their dots — readable at every zoom
  • Voice transcription stays in the active document instead of duplicating across every open window
  • Vault embeddings respect vault switches — no stale chunks leaking between vaults

item.title: Version 2.6
item.description:
cyberWriter 2.6

Performance

  • 6x faster preview rendering on large documents
  • DOM diffing (morphdom) — only changed content re-renders
  • Cached syntax highlighter eliminates repeated initialization

Polish

  • CSV/TSV files render as interactive tables in preview
  • Non-editable vault files open with system default handler
  • File extensions shown for non-text files in vault sidebar
  • Vault highlight matches by file path, not just name
  • Escape key no longer exits native fullscreen
  • Styled AI quick actions text input

item.title: Version 2.5
item.description:
cyberWriter 2.5

  • Flash Cards with SM-2 spaced repetition
  • Presentation mode: zoom, mermaid/KaTeX, title slides
  • Apple Intelligence (on-device AI, macOS 26+)
  • No more keychain password prompt on launch
  • Auto-updater fixed

item.title: Version 2.4
item.description:
⚠️ Manual download required

Please download the latest version directly.

Download from cyberwriter.app →
